baseVersion: v2 complete as of S160 on branch ralph/luminary-v2

v3Philosophy.theme: Notes become first-class knowledge citizens: organized into typed collections, connected to the entity graph, and discoverable through semantic clustering and hierarchical tags.
principles: Notes are graph nodes, not flat records. Every note saved fires an async task that upserts a Kuzu NOTE node and edges to Entity nodes via GLiNER extraction. The graph is the connective tissue; SQL is the authoritative store.

Hierarchical tags with a controlled vocabulary. Tags follow a slash-convention (science/biology/genetics). A shadow index table (NoteTagIndexModel) makes prefix-search O(1) and enables hierarchy-aware filtering. Tag normalization via embedding similarity prevents vocabulary explosion.

Collections replace group_name. Notes belong to zero or many named collections (many-to-many). Collections have metadata (description, color) and support 2-level nesting. Semantic clustering (HDBSCAN) auto-suggests collections from embedding similarity -- human approves, machine proposes.

Performance contracts are explicit. The 1024-dim vector schema must match the embedding model (bge-m3). Tag index queries must pass p95 < 100ms at 1,000 notes. Every new async path follows the established asyncio.create_task fire-and-forget pattern with explicit error logging.

Cross-tab navigation is event-driven. Clicking a tag node in the Viz tab fires a 'luminary:navigate' DOM event; App.tsx routes to Notes tab with the tag pre-applied. No URL hacks, no shared global state beyond Zustand store.
Every new service method and every new API endpoint must have at least one pytest test. No exceptions (inherited from v2 philosophy).

stories.title: Note collections: schema, API, and group_name migration
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: NoteModel.group_name is a freeform string giving each note exactly one optional group label with no metadata, no nesting, and no many-to-many support. As notes grow past a few dozen, users need named collections with descriptions and colors, the ability to assign one note to multiple collections, and optional parent-child nesting (e.g., Learning > Physics > Quantum). group_name is deprecated in this story but kept as a nullable column for backward compatibility. USER JOURNEY: User has 80 notes scattered across 12 distinct group_name values -> opens Notes tab -> sees new 'Collections' sidebar section -> clicks 'Create collection' -> creates 'Quantum Mechanics' under parent collection 'Physics' -> assigns existing notes via checkbox panel -> sidebar shows 'Physics (45)' expandable to 'Quantum Mechanics (18)'. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: None from prior Phase 13 stories. NoteModel.group_name (S87 NoteEditorDialog) preserved as nullable -- do not remove the column. GET /notes groups endpoint (S89) remains functional; add collection_id filter alongside. BACKEND: NoteCollectionModel in models.py: id (UUID PK), name (str required), description (str nullable), color (str hex default '#6366F1'), icon (str nullable, emoji), parent_collection_id (FK to self nullable), sort_order (int default 0), created_at, updated_at. NoteCollectionMemberModel pivot: note_id (cascade delete), collection_id (cascade delete), added_at, composite PK (note_id, collection_id). db_init.py migration: CREATE TABLE note_collections; CREATE TABLE note_collection_members; SELECT DISTINCT group_name FROM notes WHERE group_name IS NOT NULL; INSERT INTO note_collections(id, name, color) for each distinct value using a generated UUID; INSERT INTO note_collection_members SELECT id, <new_collection_id> FROM notes WHERE group_name = <value>. Router routers/collections.py (new file): GET /collections (flat list with note_count), GET /collections/tree (nested JSON, children array), POST /collections, PUT /collections/{id}, DELETE /collections/{id} (cascades members, not notes), POST /collections/{id}/notes (add note, ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING), DELETE /collections/{id}/notes/{note_id}. Nesting constraint: POST /collections returns 422 when the requested parent_collection_id itself has a parent (max depth = 2). GET /notes gains optional collection_id query param: adds JOIN note_collection_members WHERE collection_id = :cid. Register router in main.py. CollectionSchema TypedDict: id, name, description, color, icon, parent_collection_id, sort_order, note_count, children (tree endpoint only). TESTS: Unit test: collection at depth 3 returns 422. Unit test: DELETE /collections/{id} cascades pivot rows; notes survive. Unit test: migration inserts one collection per distinct group_name with correct membership. Unit test: GET /notes?collection_id={id} returns only notes in that collection via pivot join.
acceptanceCriteria: NoteCollectionModel in models.py: id, name, description, color ('#6366F1' default), icon, parent_collection_id (self-FK nullable), sort_order, created_at, updated_at
NoteCollectionMemberModel pivot: composite PK (note_id, collection_id), cascade deletes on both FKs, added_at timestamp
db_init.py migration: note_collections and note_collection_members tables created; all distinct group_name values migrated as top-level collections with correct note memberships
POST /collections with a parent whose parent_collection_id is already set returns 422 (max 2-level nesting)
DELETE /collections/{id} cascades to note_collection_members rows; linked notes are NOT deleted
GET /collections/tree returns nested JSON {id, name, color, note_count, children: [...]}; leaf collections have children: []
GET /notes?collection_id={id} returns only notes belonging to that collection via NoteCollectionMemberModel join
POST /collections/{id}/notes silently ignores duplicate membership (ON CONFLICT DO NOTHING)
NoteModel.group_name column preserved; existing groups endpoint (GET /notes/groups) continues to work
Unit test: migration preserves all distinct group_name values and note memberships
Unit test: tree endpoint nests correctly for 2-level fixture (parent with 2 children)

stories.title: Hierarchical tags: shadow index table, canonical tag registry, prefix-search API
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: Tags are stored as flat JSON arrays in notes.tags with no canonical registry, no hierarchy, and no SQL index -- tag filtering via json_each(notes.tags) is O(n) over the full notes table. PKM research on 8,000-note vaults confirms flat tag systems break at scale: synonym explosion ('ml', 'machine-learning', 'Machine Learning') and lack of browse hierarchy (clicking 'science' should match 'science/biology'). This story adds: (1) NoteTagIndexModel -- a shadow/denorm table that explodes each note's tags into indexed rows for O(1) lookup; (2) CanonicalTagModel -- a registry supporting slash-convention parent-child hierarchy ('science/biology/genetics'); (3) a full tag API including prefix-search autocomplete; (4) hierarchical matching in GET /notes?tag= (query for 'science' also returns 'science/biology' and 'science/physics/quantum' notes). USER JOURNEY: User types 'prog' in tag filter -> autocomplete shows 'programming', 'programming/python', 'programming/go' -> clicks 'programming' -> all programming/* notes shown in main pane. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: GET /notes tag filter (S89) rewritten to use NoteTagIndexModel prefix query. Note create/update/delete routes (S89) must call _sync_tag_index synchronously. The existing suggest-tags endpoint (S89) returns flat strings; S162 registers them into CanonicalTagModel on upsert. BACKEND: NoteTagIndexModel in models.py: note_id (str, no FK -- shadow table to avoid FK overhead), tag_full (str e.g. 'science/biology'), tag_root (str first segment 'science'), tag_parent (str all-but-last segment 'science', empty string if top-level). Unique composite on (note_id, tag_full). CanonicalTagModel: id = tag_full slug (PK), display_name (str), parent_tag (str nullable, FK to self), note_count (int denorm), created_at. TagAliasModel: alias (str PK), canonical_tag_id (FK). Sync function _sync_tag_index(note_id, tags, db) in routers/notes.py: called synchronously on note create/update/delete. On create/update: DELETE FROM note_tag_index WHERE note_id = :nid; INSERT for each tag in tags; UPDATE CanonicalTagModel note_count (+1 for new, -1 for removed). On delete: DELETE all rows for note_id; decrement counts. Router routers/tags.py (new file): GET /tags (flat list sorted note_count DESC, fields: id, display_name, note_count, parent_tag), GET /tags/tree (nested with children), GET /tags/{tag_id}/notes (notes with this tag including children), GET /tags/autocomplete?q= (LIKE :q || '%' on NoteTagIndexModel.tag_full, up to 10 results), POST /tags (create canonical), PUT /tags/{tag_id} (rename display_name or re-parent), DELETE /tags/{tag_id} (409 if note_count > 0). GET /notes?tag= rewritten: WHERE note_id IN (SELECT note_id FROM note_tag_index WHERE tag_full = :tag OR tag_full LIKE :tag || '/%'). Register router in main.py. db_init.py migration: CREATE TABLE note_tag_index; CREATE TABLE canonical_tags; CREATE TABLE tag_aliases; CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_note_tag_index_tag_full ON note_tag_index(tag_full); CREATE INDEX IF NOT EXISTS idx_note_tag_index_note_id ON note_tag_index(note_id); backfill: SELECT id, tags FROM notes; call _sync logic in pure SQL for existing rows. TESTS: Unit test: note with tags ['programming/python', 'programming/go'] produces 2 NoteTagIndexModel rows with tag_root='programming'. Unit test: GET /notes?tag=programming returns notes tagged 'programming', 'programming/python', 'programming/go'. Unit test: deleting a note removes its NoteTagIndexModel rows and decrements CanonicalTagModel.note_count. Unit test: autocomplete for 'prog' returns 'programming' and its children sorted by note_count.
acceptanceCriteria: NoteTagIndexModel in models.py: note_id, tag_full, tag_root, tag_parent; composite unique (note_id, tag_full)
CanonicalTagModel: id (slug PK), display_name, parent_tag (self-FK nullable), note_count (int), created_at
TagAliasModel: alias (str PK), canonical_tag_id (FK to CanonicalTagModel)
_sync_tag_index(note_id, tags, db) called synchronously in note create, update, and delete routes; keeps NoteTagIndexModel and CanonicalTagModel.note_count accurate
db_init.py migration: note_tag_index, canonical_tags, tag_aliases tables created; indexes on tag_full and note_id; backfill from existing notes.tags
GET /notes?tag= uses NoteTagIndexModel prefix query: tag_full = :tag OR LIKE :tag || '/%'
GET /tags/autocomplete?q= returns up to 10 results by LIKE prefix, with display_name and parent_tag breadcrumb
GET /tags/tree returns hierarchical structure; node note_count includes descendants
DELETE /tags/{id} returns 409 when note_count > 0
Unit test: filter on parent tag 'programming' returns notes tagged 'programming/python' and 'programming/go'
Unit test: note deletion removes NoteTagIndexModel rows and decrements CanonicalTagModel.note_count to 0

stories.title: Notes as Kuzu graph nodes: NOTE type, WRITTEN_ABOUT entity edges, TAG_IS_CONCEPT bridge
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: Notes exist entirely outside the Kuzu knowledge graph. The only bridge is the transient chat notes_node which retrieves note text via NoteSearchService at query time -- there are no persistent graph edges from notes to entities. This means the Viz knowledge graph shows no notes, note discovery through graph traversal is impossible, and queries like 'find all notes mentioning entities related to Gradient Descent' require a full-text scan instead of a graph traversal. This story adds three structural additions to Kuzu: NOTE node type, WRITTEN_ABOUT edges (NOTE -> Entity, auto-extracted via GLiNER on note save), and TAG_IS_CONCEPT edges (NOTE -> Entity, when a tag matches an existing Entity.name). DERIVED_FROM edges (NOTE -> Document) link notes to source books. USER JOURNEY: User saves a note about 'backpropagation' while reading a deep learning book -> async background task runs GLiNER, finds existing Entity 'Backpropagation' in Kuzu, creates WRITTEN_ABOUT edge; tag 'neural-networks' matches Entity 'neural-networks', creates TAG_IS_CONCEPT edge -> user opens Viz tab -> note node appears connected to both entities. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: POST /notes, PUT /notes/{id}, DELETE /notes/{id} (S89) each gain an asyncio.create_task call (upsert or delete). chat_graph.py notes_node (S79) upgraded: after NoteSearchService.search(), enriches context snippets with Kuzu entity names via get_entities_for_note(). Kuzu schema adds 3 edge tables and 1 node table to existing graph (S77 introduced the schema). BACKEND: graph.py _create_schema() additions: NODE TABLE Note(id STRING, note_id STRING, preview STRING, created_at STRING) PRIMARY KEY(id). REL TABLE WRITTEN_ABOUT(FROM Note TO Entity) {confidence: FLOAT}. REL TABLE TAG_IS_CONCEPT(FROM Note TO Entity) {tag: STRING}. REL TABLE DERIVED_FROM(FROM Note TO Document). NoteGraphService in app/services/note_graph.py: async upsert_note_node(note_id, content, document_id, tags, db): (1) Upsert Note node (id=note_id, preview=content[:200], created_at=now ISO string). (2) If document_id: upsert DERIVED_FROM edge to Document node. (3) Run GLiNER on content for PERSON, ORGANIZATION, CONCEPT, TECHNOLOGY entity types. For each extracted entity: run Kuzu query to look up Entity by name (case-insensitive LOWER() comparison); guard with has_next() before get_next() -- do not call get_next() without has_next() check (Kuzu raises on empty result). If found: upsert WRITTEN_ABOUT edge with confidence = entity_score. (4) For each tag: Kuzu lookup Entity where LOWER(name) = LOWER(tag); if found: upsert TAG_IS_CONCEPT edge. Called from notes router as asyncio.create_task (fire-and-forget, same pattern as vector embeddings). async delete_note_node(note_id): DELETE FROM Note WHERE note_id = :nid (Kuzu cascades incident edges on node delete). Called synchronously in DELETE /notes/{id} handler before note row deletion. async get_entities_for_note(note_id): Cypher MATCH (n:Note)-[r:WRITTEN_ABOUT|TAG_IS_CONCEPT]->(e:Entity) WHERE n.note_id = $nid RETURN e.name, e.type, r.confidence, type(r). Guard each get_next() with has_next(). async get_notes_for_entity(entity_name): MATCH (n:Note)-[:WRITTEN_ABOUT|:TAG_IS_CONCEPT]->(e:Entity) WHERE LOWER(e.name) = LOWER($name) RETURN n.note_id. GET /notes/{note_id}/entities: calls get_entities_for_note, returns [{name, type, confidence, edge_type}]. chat_graph.py notes_node: after NoteSearchService results, for top-3 results call get_entities_for_note(note_id) and append entity names to context chunk metadata. TESTS: Unit test: upsert_note_node for note whose content matches an existing Entity creates WRITTEN_ABOUT edge; entity absent from Kuzu is skipped without error. Unit test: note with document_id creates DERIVED_FROM edge to correct Document node. Unit test: tag matching existing Entity.name creates TAG_IS_CONCEPT edge. Unit test: delete_note_node removes Note node; subsequent get_entities_for_note returns []. Unit test: get_notes_for_entity returns correct note_id for a known entity.
acceptanceCriteria: Kuzu schema in graph.py: Note node table (id, note_id, preview, created_at), WRITTEN_ABOUT rel (Note->Entity, confidence FLOAT), TAG_IS_CONCEPT rel (Note->Entity, tag STRING), DERIVED_FROM rel (Note->Document)
NoteGraphService in app/services/note_graph.py with upsert_note_node, delete_note_node, get_entities_for_note, get_notes_for_entity
Every Kuzu get_next() call is guarded by has_next() -- no bare get_next() on potentially empty results
upsert_note_node called as asyncio.create_task on POST /notes and PUT /notes/{id}
delete_note_node called synchronously in DELETE /notes/{id} handler
WRITTEN_ABOUT edges created for GLiNER-extracted entities found in Kuzu Entity table; absent entities silently skipped
TAG_IS_CONCEPT edges created when a note tag matches an Entity.name (case-insensitive)
DERIVED_FROM edge created when note has non-null document_id and Document node exists
GET /notes/{note_id}/entities returns [{name, type, confidence, edge_type}]
chat_graph.py notes_node appends Kuzu entity names to top-3 note context snippets
Unit test: WRITTEN_ABOUT edge created for known entity; absent entity skipped without exception
Unit test: delete_note_node; get_entities_for_note returns [] for deleted note

stories.title: Notes performance refactor: fix vector dimension mismatch (384->1024), add tag indexes, batch reindex
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: Three technical debts in the notes subsystem must be resolved before Phase 1 work scales to large note corpora. (1) VECTOR DIM BUG: The LanceDB note_vectors_v2 table schema is defined with pa.list_(pa.float32(), 384) in vector_store.py but BAAI/bge-m3 produces 1024-dim embeddings -- a silent mismatch that causes vector upserts to fail or produce truncated vectors, making semantic note search unreliable. (2) TAG QUERY INDEXES: S162 added NoteTagIndexModel with indexes in db_init.py. This story adds a @pytest.mark.slow performance regression guard to verify those indexes hold at 1,000-note scale. (3) ORPHANED EMBEDDINGS: Notes created before the vector store was initialized, or during Ollama downtime, have no embedding; there is no way to identify or backfill them without a dedicated endpoint. USER JOURNEY: Admin (developer) calls POST /admin/notes/reindex -> {queued: True, total_notes: 247} -> background task re-embeds 31 notes with missing or mismatched vectors -> GET /notes/search now returns semantically relevant results for all notes. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: vector_store.py (S89) is modified -- existing notes may have 384-dim vectors that will be erased by the migration; re-embedding all notes is required. NoteTagIndexModel indexes (S162) are verified under load here. BACKEND: NOTE_VECTOR_DIM = 1024 constant in vector_store.py. _get_or_create_note_table(): inspect existing LanceDB table schema (table.schema, check the vector field list_size via PyArrow field type). If list_size != 1024: log warning 'note_vectors_v2 schema mismatch (found {actual_dim}-dim) -- dropping and recreating with 1024-dim', call tbl.drop() (or the LanceDB equivalent for table deletion -- use db.drop_table('note_vectors_v2') via the lancedb connection object), then recreate. This detection runs at startup (called from get_vector_store()). ReindexService in app/services/reindex_service.py: async reindex_notes(db: AsyncSession) -> ReindexReport TypedDict {total: int, reindexed: int, failed: int}: fetch all NoteModel rows (id, content). For each: check if note_id present in LanceDB (synchronous tbl.search(query_vector).where(f"note_id = '{nid}'").limit(1).to_pandas() -- wrap sync call in asyncio.to_thread). If absent: re-embed content and upsert. Catch embedding exceptions per-note; increment failed. Return report. Run entire reindex as asyncio.create_task. POST /admin/notes/reindex: X-Admin-Key header checked against settings.ADMIN_KEY (env var, default empty string = no auth for local dev). If ADMIN_KEY non-empty and header missing or wrong: return 403. Returns {queued: True, total_notes: int}. PERFORMANCE TEST (@pytest.mark.slow, guarded by LUMINARY_PERF_TESTS env var per patterns.md): insert 1,000 synthetic NoteModel + NoteTagIndexModel rows via fixture. Run GET /notes?tag=science 10 times via test client. Assert p95 < 100ms (use time.perf_counter). TESTS: Unit test: _get_or_create_note_table with a mock 384-dim schema triggers drop-and-recreate warning. Unit test: reindex_notes identifies notes absent from LanceDB and returns correct counts in ReindexReport. Unit test: POST /admin/notes/reindex with wrong X-Admin-Key returns 403 when ADMIN_KEY is set to a non-empty value.
acceptanceCriteria: vector_store.py defines NOTE_VECTOR_DIM = 1024; _get_or_create_note_table detects mismatched schema and drops+recreates with correct dim at startup
Warning logged when dim migration triggers: includes actual dim found and target dim
ReindexService in app/services/reindex_service.py: async reindex_notes returns ReindexReport {total, reindexed, failed}; sync LanceDB calls wrapped in asyncio.to_thread
POST /admin/notes/reindex triggers reindex_notes as asyncio.create_task; returns {queued: True, total_notes: int}
POST /admin/notes/reindex returns 403 when X-Admin-Key does not match non-empty settings.ADMIN_KEY
Unit test: dim mismatch detection triggers drop-and-recreate path
Unit test: reindex_notes returns correct reindexed count for notes absent from LanceDB
Unit test: wrong admin key returns 403; empty ADMIN_KEY allows all requests
@pytest.mark.slow test (guarded by LUMINARY_PERF_TESTS env var): 1,000-note tag query p95 < 100ms with NoteTagIndexModel indexes from S162

stories.title: Collections UI: sidebar tree, CRUD dialog, multi-collection assignment in note editor
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: S161 adds the backend collections system. This story builds the React frontend: a collapsible collection tree in the Notes sidebar replacing the flat group_name filter list, a create/edit/delete collection dialog, and multi-collection assignment checkboxes in NoteEditorDialog. The UX follows Obsidian's folder panel pattern (collapsible items, count badges, icon-triggered actions) within Luminary's existing shadcn/ui + Tailwind conventions. Notes can belong to multiple collections simultaneously. USER JOURNEY: User opens Notes tab -> sidebar shows 'Collections' header with '+' button -> 3 top-level collections with children -> user clicks 'Physics' to expand -> sees 'Quantum Mechanics (18)' and 'Thermodynamics (27)' -> clicks 'Quantum Mechanics' -> main pane filters to 18 notes -> clicks a note pencil icon -> NoteEditorDialog opens with 'Collections' section showing checkbox list -> user checks 'Study Targets' -> note now in both collections without clicking Save (collection membership saves immediately on check). CROSS-STORY IMPACT: NoteEditorDialog (S87) gains a new 'Collections' section below the existing tags section. GET /notes/{note_id} (S89) must return collection_ids array -- add this field to the note response schema. Notes sidebar 'Groups' section (S89) replaced by 'Collections'. group_name is deprecated: existing NoteEditorDialog group_name input (S87) should be removed. FRONTEND: CollectionTree component (frontend/src/components/CollectionTree.tsx): renders GET /collections/tree. Each CollectionTreeItem: colored square dot (collection.color), chevron if children present, name, note_count pill. Click = setActiveCollectionId in useAppStore; Notes page refetches with ?collection_id=. Expand/collapse via local useState. Rename: inline input on double-click or pencil icon; PUT /collections/{id} on Enter or blur. Delete: trash icon opens confirm AlertDialog ('Delete collection? Notes are not deleted.'); DELETE /collections/{id} on confirm. CreateCollectionDialog (frontend/src/components/CreateCollectionDialog.tsx): name input (required), description textarea, 8-swatch color picker (hex values), parent collection select (top-level only from GET /collections flat). POST /collections on save. Notes sidebar: replace 'Groups' section with 'Collections' label + CollectionTree + 'New Collection' button at bottom. NoteEditorDialog: add 'Collections' section below tags. Renders scrollable max-h-40 list of all collections (GET /collections flat). Checkbox per collection; pre-checked based on note's collection_ids (add collection_ids field to note response). On check: POST /collections/{id}/notes immediately. On uncheck: DELETE /collections/{id}/notes/{note_id} immediately. No save button for collection changes. GET /notes and GET /notes/{id} response: add collection_ids: list[str] field by joining NoteCollectionMemberModel for each note. useAppStore: add activeCollectionId (string | null), setActiveCollectionId. Notes page useQuery: passes collection_id param when activeCollectionId set. HTML5 drag-and-drop on note table rows: onDragStart stores note_id; CollectionTree items accept onDragOver/onDrop; drop fires POST /collections/{id}/notes. No DnD library needed. STATES: CollectionTree loading: 3 skeleton lines. Empty: 'Create your first collection' placeholder with arrow pointing to '+'. Error: 'Could not load collections' with retry. NoteEditorDialog collections loading: skeleton checkboxes. TESTS: Vitest: CollectionTree renders correct item count and nesting from mock GET /collections/tree fixture. Vitest: checkbox check fires POST /collections/{id}/notes; uncheck fires DELETE with correct ids. Vitest: confirm dialog shows correct collection name before delete.

stories.title: Hierarchical tag browser UI: tree sidebar, tag management panel, NoteEditorDialog autocomplete
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: S162 adds the hierarchical tag backend (NoteTagIndexModel, CanonicalTagModel, prefix-search API). This story builds the frontend counterpart: a hierarchical tag tree in the Notes sidebar, a tag management panel (rename, re-parent, merge), and an upgraded NoteEditorDialog tag input with hierarchy-aware autocomplete. The current flat chip input (S87) does not communicate hierarchy to the user; a tag like 'programming/python' is entered and displayed as an opaque string. After this story the UI navigates the hierarchy visually and helps users stay within the controlled vocabulary. USER JOURNEY: User opens Notes sidebar -> 'Tags' section shows 'programming (34)' collapsible -> expands to 'python (18)', 'go (9)', 'rust (7)' -> clicks 'programming' -> all programming/* notes displayed -> hovers 'ml' tag -> gear icon -> 'Merge into...' combobox -> types 'machine-learning' -> accepts -> 34 notes updated. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: NoteEditorDialog tag input (S87) is replaced by TagAutocomplete component. The current flat string input must be removed and the new component integrated while preserving the existing tag chip display (chip X-to-remove, comma/Enter to add -- same feel, different input). Note card list (S89) tag display updated from flat chips to breadcrumb style. FRONTEND: TagTree component (frontend/src/components/TagTree.tsx): renders GET /tags/tree as collapsible nested list. TagTreeItem: indent proportional to depth, tag name, note_count pill. Click item: sets activeTag in useAppStore; Notes page refetches with ?tag=. Gear icon on hover: opens TagManagementPanel popover. Merge Into: GET /tags/autocomplete live results, POST /tags/merge {source_tag_id, target_tag_id} on confirm. POST /tags/merge is a new backend endpoint: for each NoteModel where source_tag in tags array (json_each), replace source with target and deduplicate, call _sync_tag_index, insert TagAliasModel row (source -> target), delete source CanonicalTagModel. Returns {affected_notes: int}. TagAutocomplete component (frontend/src/components/TagAutocomplete.tsx): replaces flat input in NoteEditorDialog. Debounced (200ms) GET /tags/autocomplete?q={input}. Dropdown: each result shows full slug as primary text and parent breadcrumb as muted secondary text. Selecting inserts the full slug into the tags list. Pressing '/' at end of a root token (e.g. 'programming/') fires GET /tags/autocomplete?q=programming/ to show children only. Arrow keys navigate; Enter selects; Escape closes. Chip display unchanged (X to remove). Notes sidebar: add 'Tags' section below 'Collections'; render TagTree and note count. useAppStore: add activeTag (string | null), setActiveTag. Notes page: when activeTag set, passes ?tag= to GET /notes (hierarchical prefix match). Note cards in list/table view: display tags as 'root/child' breadcrumb style (root in text-primary, /child in text-muted-foreground) instead of flat opaque chips. stories.title: Tag co-occurrence graph in Viz tab: Sigma.js tag network mode with cross-tab note filter
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: The Viz tab renders the book entity graph via Sigma.js v3 + Graphology but shows no note-derived structure. This story adds a 'Tags' graph mode: nodes are canonical tags (sized by note_count, colored by parent tag family), edges are co-occurrences (two tags appearing on the same note, weighted by co-occurrence count). The graph reveals how the user's personal knowledge base clusters -- distinct from the entity graph which reflects book content. Clicking a tag node fires a cross-tab navigation event that activates the Notes tab with that tag applied as a filter. The graph is generated from NoteTagIndexModel (S162) with a cap of 200 nodes and 500 edges for rendering clarity. USER JOURNEY: User opens Viz tab -> clicks 'Tags' mode -> tag network renders: large 'machine-learning' node connected to 'neural-networks', 'optimization', 'backpropagation'; separate cluster 'history' -> 'ancient-rome', 'empire' -> user clicks 'machine-learning' -> Notes tab activates with tag filter applied, showing all ml/* notes. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: Viz tab mode selector (S112 added entity graph modes) gains a new 'Tags' button. S162 NoteTagIndexModel is the data source. Notes page activeTag (S165 useAppStore field) is the cross-tab target. BACKEND: TagGraphService in app/services/tag_graph.py: async build_tag_graph(db: AsyncSession) -> TagGraphData TypedDict {nodes: list[TagNode], edges: list[TagEdge], generated_at: str}. TagNode: {id, label, note_count, parent_tag, color}. TagEdge: {source, target, weight}. Implementation: (1) Fetch top-200 CanonicalTagModel by note_count where note_count > 0. (2) Co-occurrence SQL: SELECT a.tag_full, b.tag_full, COUNT(*) as weight FROM note_tag_index a JOIN note_tag_index b ON a.note_id = b.note_id AND a.tag_full < b.tag_full WHERE a.tag_full IN (:top_200) AND b.tag_full IN (:top_200) GROUP BY 1, 2 HAVING COUNT(*) >= 2 ORDER BY weight DESC LIMIT 500. (3) Node color: deterministic from parent_tag -- hash parent_tag string to index 0-11, map to 12-color palette (hex constants in service). Nodes with no parent use a default grey. In-memory LRU invalidation: TagGraphService caches the result dict; cache is cleared in _sync_tag_index whenever any tag row is modified. GET /tags/graph: calls build_tag_graph; returns TagGraphData. TypedDicts in app/types/. FRONTEND: Viz tab: add 'Tags' button to mode selector. TagGraph component (frontend/src/components/viz/TagGraph.tsx): import Sigma + Graphology (same pattern as existing VizGraph). Build Graphology graph: nodes with size = Math.max(10, Math.sqrt(noteCount) * 6), edges with weight. Apply ForceAtlas2 layout (same settings as existing graph). On Sigma node click: dispatch custom DOM event 'luminary:navigate' with detail {tab: 'notes', tagFilter: nodeId}. App.tsx useEffect: listen for 'luminary:navigate'; on fire, setActiveTab to 'notes' and call setActiveTag(tagFilter) in useAppStore. Notes page: on mount, read activeTag from store and pre-populate tag filter (same mechanism as S165 activeTag). STATES: TagGraph loading: centered Spinner. Empty (< 3 unique tags): 'Take more notes to see your tag network' message. Error: inline 'Could not load tag graph' with retry button. TESTS: Unit test: build_tag_graph with 4-note fixture sharing known tag pairs returns correct edge weights. Unit test: TagGraphService cache is cleared after _sync_tag_index modifies a tag row. Vitest: TagGraph renders correct node count from mock GET /tags/graph fixture. Vitest: node click dispatches luminary:navigate event with correct tagFilter value.

stories.title: Smart tag normalization: embedding similarity scan, merge suggestions, accept/reject drawer
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: Even with hierarchical tags (S162), users accumulate synonymous tags across notes from different books and sessions: 'ml', 'machine-learning', 'Machine Learning', 'AI', 'artificial-intelligence'. The LLM tagger (S89) generates new tags per-note without checking the existing controlled vocabulary. PKM research on 8,000-note vaults found 2,141 unique tags -- roughly 1 per 4 notes -- an unmanageable vocabulary that defeats the purpose of tagging. This story adds a SmartTagNormalizerService that embeds all canonical tag names using bge-m3, finds semantically similar pairs above a 0.85 cosine similarity threshold, and surfaces merge suggestions in a NormalizationDrawer where the user reviews and accepts or rejects each pair. USER JOURNEY: User clicks 'Normalize' (wrench icon) in Notes sidebar Tags section -> scan runs -> drawer opens: 6 suggestions including 'ml -- machine-learning (91% similar, 34 notes)' -> user accepts 4, rejects 2 -> 62 notes updated with canonical tags. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: POST /tags/merge (S165) is reused by the accept action. TagAliasModel and CanonicalTagModel (S162) are the target schema. _sync_tag_index (S162) is called by merge for each updated note. The NoteTagIndexModel cache invalidation (S167 TagGraphService) must be triggered by the merge operation. BACKEND: TagMergeSuggestionModel in models.py: id (UUID PK), tag_a_id (str, FK CanonicalTagModel.id), tag_b_id (str), similarity (float), suggested_canonical_id (str -- whichever of the pair has higher note_count), status ('pending'|'accepted'|'rejected'), created_at. db_init.py: CREATE TABLE tag_merge_suggestions. SmartTagNormalizerService in app/services/tag_normalizer.py: async scan(db) -> int: fetch all CanonicalTagModel rows. Embed display_names using the same embedding model as the retriever -- call the embeddings function used in NoteSearchService (e.g. embed a list of strings via the ONNX bge-m3 session, same path as chunk/note embedding). Compute pairwise cosine similarity as numpy dot product on L2-normalised vectors. For pairs with similarity > 0.85 where neither is an alias of the other (check TagAliasModel): insert TagMergeSuggestionModel row; suggested_canonical = whichever has higher note_count. Return count of new suggestions created. POST /tags/normalization/scan: triggers scan as asyncio.create_task; returns {queued: True}. GET /tags/normalization/suggestions: returns pending TagMergeSuggestionModel rows, each with tag_a {id, display_name, note_count} and tag_b {id, display_name, note_count} expanded. POST /tags/normalization/suggestions/{id}/accept: calls existing POST /tags/merge logic (reuse, not duplicate) with tag_a_id and suggested_canonical_id; sets status=accepted; returns {affected_notes: int}. POST /tags/normalization/suggestions/{id}/reject: sets status=rejected. FRONTEND: Tags section header in Notes sidebar: 'Normalize' wrench icon button. On click: POST /tags/normalization/scan; show spinner; poll GET /tags/normalization/suggestions every 2s until suggestions arrive; open NormalizationDrawer (Sheet from right). NormalizationDrawer: each suggestion row shows TagA chip -- TagB chip, similarity badge (e.g. '91%'), '{n} notes affected'. Accept button (CheckIcon): calls POST /suggestions/{id}/accept; row transitions to green 'Merged -- {n} notes updated'. Reject button (XIcon): row fades out. When all resolved: 'All suggestions resolved -- tags are clean.' footer. STATES: Scan in progress: spinner on wrench button. No suggestions after scan: toast 'No duplicate tags found'. Drawer open with mixed states: in-flight accept shows row spinner. Error on accept: inline error on that row only; other rows unaffected. TESTS: Unit test: similarity > 0.85 between 'ml' and 'machine-learning' mock embeddings creates TagMergeSuggestionModel row; tags already linked as aliases are excluded. Unit test: SmartTagNormalizerService.scan skips pairs where TagAliasModel already links them. Unit test: accept calls merge logic, creates TagAliasModel, deletes source CanonicalTagModel, sets status=accepted.
acceptanceCriteria: TagMergeSuggestionModel in models.py: id, tag_a_id, tag_b_id, similarity (float), suggested_canonical_id, status, created_at; db_init.py migration

stories.title: Semantic note clustering via HDBSCAN: auto-suggest collections from embedding similarity
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: Note collections (S161) require manual curation -- users must create collections and assign notes themselves. At scale this is a maintenance burden. PKM research confirms that at 1,000+ notes automated organization is non-negotiable. This story adds a semantic clustering service using HDBSCAN (Hierarchical Density-Based Spatial Clustering) over note embeddings from LanceDB note_vectors_v2, generates collection name suggestions via LLM, and surfaces them in a 'Suggested Collections' sidebar panel. HDBSCAN is preferred over k-means because it handles variable cluster sizes, does not require pre-specifying cluster count, and marks noise notes (label=-1) as unclustered rather than forcing them into a group. The note_vectors_v2 table now uses 1024-dim vectors (S170 fixed the schema) so clustering produces accurate semantic groups. USER JOURNEY: User has 120 notes with minimal manual organization -> clicks 'Auto-organize' in Notes sidebar -> system clusters notes -> 7 suggested collections: 'Quantum Mechanics (18 notes)', 'Machine Learning (34 notes)', 'Ancient History (12 notes)' etc. -> user reviews each with 3-note preview -> accepts 5 -> 5 new NoteCollections created with notes assigned. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: Depends on S161 (NoteCollectionModel + NoteCollectionMemberModel created by accept). Depends on S170 (1024-dim note_vectors_v2 -- clustering only runs if vectors exist). S164 CollectionTree must refresh after accept to show new collections. BACKEND: scikit-learn >= 1.3 in pyproject.toml (uses sklearn.cluster.HDBSCAN added in scikit-learn 1.3 -- no extra hdbscan package needed). ClusterSuggestionModel in models.py: id (UUID PK), suggested_name (str), note_ids (JSON list of str), confidence_score (float, mean pairwise cosine within cluster), status ('pending'|'accepted'|'rejected'), created_at. db_init.py: CREATE TABLE cluster_suggestions. ClusteringService in app/services/clustering_service.py: async cluster_notes(db) -> int: (1) Fetch all rows from LanceDB note_vectors_v2 via synchronous to_pandas() wrapped in asyncio.to_thread; extract note_id and vector columns. (2) Build numpy matrix from vectors. If fewer than 6 notes with vectors: return 0 (not enough data). (3) Run sklearn.cluster.HDBSCAN(min_cluster_size=3, min_samples=2, metric='cosine').fit_predict(matrix). (4) For each label != -1: gather member note_ids; compute centroid; select up to 3 medoid notes (closest to centroid by cosine distance). (5) Compute confidence_score = mean pairwise cosine similarity for cluster members. (6) LLM name generation: build prompt with up to 3 note excerpts (content[:150] each). LiteLLM call: system='Given these note excerpts, suggest a 2-4 word collection name capturing their common theme. Reply with the name only.'; user=excerpts. (7) Insert ClusterSuggestionModel row. Return count of new suggestions. Rate-limit: if a ClusterSuggestionModel with status=pending exists and created_at > now - 1 hour: skip clustering; return -1. POST /notes/cluster: if rate-limited (returns -1): return {cached: True, last_run: datetime ISO str}. Else triggers cluster_notes as asyncio.create_task; returns {queued: True, total_notes: int}. GET /notes/cluster/suggestions: returns pending ClusterSuggestionModel rows sorted by confidence_score DESC; each row includes first 3 note content previews (first 100 chars of each note content fetched by note_ids). POST /notes/cluster/suggestions/{id}/accept: create NoteCollectionModel(name=suggested_name, color='#6366F1'), bulk INSERT NoteCollectionMemberModel for all note_ids, set status=accepted; return {collection_id}. POST /notes/cluster/suggestions/{id}/reject: set status=rejected. FRONTEND: Notes sidebar: 'Suggested Collections' section visible only when GET /notes/cluster/suggestions returns pending items. 'Auto-organize' button (Wand2 icon) at top of Collections section triggers POST /notes/cluster; shows spinner while queued. Each suggestion card: suggested name, '{n} notes' badge, confidence bar (filled based on confidence_score), 3 note excerpt previews as muted text. Accept (Plus icon) and Reject (X icon) buttons. On accept: POST /suggestions/{id}/accept; invalidate GET /collections/tree; suggestion card animates out. STATES: Scanning: spinner on Auto-organize button. No suggestions: 'Auto-organize' button remains visible with no panel below. Accepted suggestion: collection appears in CollectionTree (cache invalidation). TESTS: Unit test: HDBSCAN with 6-vector fixture (3 closely similar + 3 dissimilar) produces exactly 1 cluster with correct member note_ids. Unit test: noise notes (label=-1) excluded from ClusterSuggestionModel. Unit test: accept creates NoteCollection with correct note_ids as NoteCollectionMember rows. Unit test: POST /notes/cluster within 1 hour of last pending suggestion returns {cached: True}.
acceptanceCriteria: scikit-learn >= 1.3 added to pyproject.toml; uses sklearn.cluster.HDBSCAN (no separate hdbscan package)

stories.title: Collection-based flashcard generation: deck naming, incremental generation, Study tab integration
stories.phase: V3 Phase 1 - Note Organization and Knowledge Graph
stories.description: CONTEXT: Flashcard generation from notes (POST /notes/flashcards/generate, S93) accepts a tag or note_ids list. With proper collections (S161), the natural unit for a study deck is a collection: generate a deck named after the collection, covering all its notes. Incremental generation is essential: re-running should not produce duplicate cards for notes whose content has not changed. This story extends the generate endpoint to accept collection_id, names the deck after the collection, skips notes already covered by a content hash match, and surfaces collection decks as first-class options in the Study tab. USER JOURNEY: User curates 'Quantum Mechanics' collection with 18 notes -> Notes tab -> clicks 'Generate Flashcards' -> switches to 'By Collection' mode -> picks 'Quantum Mechanics' -> sees '18 notes, 0 already covered' -> clicks Generate -> 36 cards in deck 'Quantum Mechanics' -> Study tab shows 'Quantum Mechanics' deck with folder icon -> user clicks it to study. CROSS-STORY IMPACT: POST /notes/flashcards/generate (S93) extended; existing tag/note_ids paths preserved unchanged. FlashcardModel (S93) gains source_content_hash column. Study tab deck selector (S95) extended with collection decks. GET /flashcards/decks is a new endpoint. GenerateFlashcardsDialog (S93) gains 'By Collection' mode tab. BACKEND: FlashcardModel: add source_content_hash (str nullable, 16-char hex) column; db_init.py ALTER TABLE migration. FlashcardService.generate_from_collection(collection_id, count_per_note, difficulty, db): fetch note_ids from NoteCollectionMemberModel WHERE collection_id = :cid. Fetch collection NoteCollectionModel for name. For each note_id: fetch NoteModel. content_hash = sha256(note.content[:500].encode()).hexdigest()[:16]. Check: SELECT COUNT(*) FROM flashcards WHERE deck = :deck_name AND source = 'note' AND source_content_hash = :hash. If count > 0: skip (already covered). Else: call FlashcardService.generate_from_notes([note_id], count_per_note, difficulty, db); set deck = collection.name and source_content_hash = content_hash on created cards. Return {created: int, skipped: int, deck: str}. POST /notes/flashcards/generate: extend RequestBody with optional collection_id. If both collection_id and note_ids provided: 422. If collection_id: call generate_from_collection. GET /notes/flashcards/generate/preview?collection_id={id}: returns {total_notes: int, already_covered: int} without generating. already_covered = count of notes whose content_hash already matches a card in the collection deck. GET /flashcards/decks: new endpoint returning list of [{deck: str, source_type: str, card_count: int, document_id: str|None, collection_id: str|None}]. source_type = 'collection' when deck matches a NoteCollectionModel.name (join), 'document' otherwise. FRONTEND: GenerateFlashcardsDialog (Notes tab): add 'Source' Tabs component with 'By Tag' (existing content) and 'By Collection' tabs. 'By Collection' tab: CollectionTree picker (same component from S164, single-select mode). '{total_notes} notes, {already_covered} already covered' line fetched from GET /preview endpoint on collection select. 'Skip already-generated notes' checkbox (checked by default; when unchecked, skips content_hash check -- add force_regenerate: bool to request body). Study tab deck selector: call GET /flashcards/decks; render list with book icon for document decks, FolderIcon for collection decks; source badge (indigo for collection, slate for document). STATES: GenerateFlashcardsDialog 'By Collection': loading preview count (skeleton line); error ('Could not load preview'). Study tab deck list: loading (skeleton), empty ('No decks yet'), error (retry). TESTS: Unit test: generate_from_collection skips note with matching source_content_hash in same deck. Unit test: deck name equals collection.name; source_type='collection' returned by GET /flashcards/decks. Unit test: 422 returned when both collection_id and note_ids provided. Unit test: GET /preview returns correct already_covered count.

FlashcardService.generate_from_collection: fetches notes from NoteCollectionMemberModel; skips notes matching source_content_hash in same deck; sets deck = collection.name

regressionBaseline: All tests on branch ralph/luminary-v2 pass before Phase 1 implementation begins (run: uv run pytest from backend/). After each story, the test count must be >= the count at the start of that story's implementation. Any drop is a regression -- stop and fix before setting passes: true.
